title: biscutella apula var. ciliata
intro: Biscutella apula var. ciliata, also known as Biscutella, is a plant species that belongs to the family Brassicaceae. It is a flowering plant native to Italy, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant is valued for its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Biscutella apula var. ciliata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used to treat colds, fever, and other infections, while also providing antioxidant and antimicrobial benefits.
